---
pagetitle: "Schedule"

---
::: grid
::: {#sponsors_banner .g-col-12 .title}
# Course Schedule
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .full_text_block}
> **Note:** The schedule below is tentative and subject to change based on field conditions, instructor availability, and logistical considerations.

### Pre-Course (Online) | 6 - 9 July 2026

| Day | Session | Topic | Activities |
|---|----|-------|-----------|
| Day 1 | Online | Tropical Biodiversity in the Data Age | Lectures on biodiversity challenges, data types, and course overview |
| Day 2 | Online | Study Design, Sampling, & Metadata | Sampling design, metadata standards, ethics; mini exercise |
| Day 3 | Online | Genomics for Biodiversity Assesment | Barcoding, metagenomics, interpretation of diversity data |
| Day 4 | Online | From Data to Impact | Bioprospecting, GIS introduction, policy case studies |
: {.striped .hover}

### Onsite Course | 13 - 19 July 2026

| Day | Session | Topic | Activities |
|---|----|-------|-----------|
| Day 5 | Lab      | Welcoming Ceremony & Field Sampling Preparation | Opening session, course briefing, introduction to field protocols, sampling design, and metadata standards |
| Day 6 | Field    | Field Sampling Day 1 (Terrestrial Ecosystems) | Terrestrial sampling (soil, plants, insects), environmental measurements, GIS data acquisition using drones |
| Day 7 | Field    | Field Sampling Day 2 (Coastal/Intertidal) | Intertidal sampling, water sampling, ecological observations, metadata recording |
| Day 8 | Lab      | From Sample to DNA | DNA extraction, quality control (QC), Library Preparation |
| Day 9 | Computer | Bioinformatics for Biodiversity | Sequence data processing, taxonomic profiling, diversity analysis |
| Day 10 | Computer | From Genomes to Landscapes | Integrating biodiversity data with GIS and spatial mapping |
| Day 11 | Summit  | From Data to Decisions | Group presentations, translating results into policy recommendations, discussion & closing |
: {.striped .hover}

:::
